{"step_by_step_thinking": "Based on the provided documents, the periodontal fibers that are consistent and can be reconstructed even after the destruction of the alveolar bone are the transseptal fibers. The transseptal fibers connect the roots of adjacent teeth and are not dependent on the alveolar bone for their function. They are resilient and can be re-established even if the alveolar bone is destroyed.", "answer_choice": "D"}
